title: Impact of tree canopy cover on the local thermal environments at the Angkor temples of Cambodia | Scientific Reports
highlights: Study area description  The precincts of the selected temples (AW, BK, and TP) exhibit a common distribution of tree species that reflect the climatic environment. The main species that formed the canopy are Capparis micracantha, Delonix regia, Diospyros decandra, Ficus altissima, Irvingia malayana, Limonia acidissima L., and Tetrameles nudiflora. Borassus flabellifer was also observed at AW (Fig. 1).  ...    direction and 985 m in the east–west direction (Fig. 1). The sanctuary (Fig. S1 TP6, TP8) is at the center of the precinct, and it has a rectangular enclosure measuring approximately 150 m on each side. - Blog  ...    # Tree roots envelop some of the temples in mysterious Angkor, Cambodia. 22 Mar 2019  ...    Posted on 22 Mar 2019 Ancient Khmer temple ruins amid tree roots at Angkor Wat, Cambodia. Photo: Frolova_Elena/Shutterstock Ancient Khmer temple ruins amid tree roots at Angkor Wat, Cambodia. Photo: Frolova_Elena/Shutterstock  ...    Built in the early 12th century, the amazing temple city of Angkor Wat has long been a symbol of Cambodia. Having fallen into decline, during the 1970s civil war and Khmer Rouge control the structures were stripped of firewood and art. Banyan tree roots have grown over their facades and through existing cracks into the structures. The site was designated a UNESCO world heritage site in 1992.
